---
title: "How photon emission is set up on the A=1 lattice"
description: >-
  Methods exposition for the photon-emission experiment: what we
  measure, how the lattice is configured to emit a photon, how to
  reproduce the run.
author: "Jack D. Menendez"
date: "2026-05-20"
draft: true
categories: [essay, methods, photons, experiment]
---

<!--
Drafting notes (delete before publishing):

- Stage: drafts/ directory. Excluded from the live site.
- When ready, move this directory to essays/posts/ and set
  `draft: false`.
- This essay is methods-shaped (not discovery-shaped). The
  structure below mirrors a small experimental paper's M&M
  section: what the observable is, how the lattice is configured
  to produce it, what the runtime evidence looks like, how a
  reader reproduces it.
- Identify the actual exp_NN script before publishing.  Likely
  candidates (verify by reading the script docstrings):
    - exp_03_lanterns (lantern / radiation patterns)
    - exp_harmonic / exp_harmonic_hires (harmonic spectrum)
    - any exp_NN in the repo whose docstring mentions photon
      emission / radiation explicitly
  Drop the figure (e.g. exp_03_lanterns.png from
  c:/dev/dcl/figures/) into this directory and rename to a
  fig-*.png convention.
-->

## What we measure

> *TODO:* define the observable in one paragraph.  What counts as
> "a photon" on the lattice — a localised excitation that
> propagates at the photon ($\omega=0$) cone with no mass-shell
> displacement?  What do we record (escape time, energy spectrum,
> spatial distribution)?  Be specific enough that a reader can
> identify the observable in the script output without ambiguity.

## The experiment in one paragraph

> *TODO:* which `exp_NN` script in which repo realises this.  What
> does it scan (a frequency parameter, a coupling, an initial
> condition)?  What does it hold fixed?  Cite the exact script
> path, e.g. [`external/dcl/experiments/exp_03_lanterns.py`](https://github.com/JackDMenendez/dcl/blob/main/experiments/exp_03_lanterns.py).

## The setup

### Initial conditions

> *TODO:* the lattice configuration.  Grid size, boundary
> conditions, basis (RGB vs CMY sublattice), and the source term
> that seeds the emission.  Include the actual config block if
> short:

```python
# Replace with the real config; example shape only.
config = {
    "grid":           (51, 51, 51),
    "tick_rule":      "A1_bipartite",
    "source":         {"type": "localised", "centre": (25, 25, 25)},
    "omega":          0.0,        # photon: massless on the optical axis
    "n_ticks":        14,
    "diagnostics":    ["spectral_power", "screen_image"],
}
```

### What varies

> *TODO:* the parameter scan or initial-condition set.  If the
> essay is a methods exposition for a single representative run,
> just describe that run.  If it's a scan, list the axes
> (e.g. $\omega \in [0, \pi/4]$ in steps of $\pi/64$) and the
> total runtime.

### What we record

> *TODO:* the columns of the output table / the keys of the
> output dict.  Reference the script's `save_diagnostics(...)` or
> equivalent.

## What we see

> *TODO:* the result.  Drop the figure here.

<!-- Example image reference (replace once figure is added):

![Spectral power on the screen at tick 14: the lantern pattern of a single emitted photon, viewed in projection onto the invariant plane.](fig-lantern-pattern.png){fig-alt="Heatmap of spectral power on a 2D projection screen at lattice tick 14. A bright central region radiates outward with hexagonal symmetry; faint secondary lobes mark Arnold-tongue side-bands."}

-->

> *TODO:* the prose reading.  What does the picture *show* — the
> central lobe is the on-cone photon; the side-bands are the
> off-cone Arnold-tongue contributions; etc.  One paragraph
> minimum, two if the picture has structure worth pointing at.

## What this tests

> *TODO:* which audit row in Paper~I (or downstream) does this
> realise?  PASS / PART / STUB / FAIL — what does this run say
> about that row?  This is the load-bearing connection to the
> framework's scorecard and should not be skipped.

## Reproducing it

> *TODO:* the literal commands.  Repo, pinned commit / tag,
> environment, the exact invocation:

```sh
git clone https://github.com/JackDMenendez/dcl
cd dcl
git checkout <pinned-tag>
python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate  # or activate.bat on Windows
pip install -e .
python experiments/exp_03_lanterns.py --omega 0.0 --n-ticks 14
```

> *TODO:* expected runtime, output location (which file under
> `figures/` or `data/`), and any GPU / memory requirements.

## References

- Paper~I — *Geometry First* (the observational target this
  experiment supports).  doi:[10.5281/zenodo.20078529](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20078529)
- Script: `external/dcl/experiments/<exp_NN>.py` *(fill in)*.
- `dcl-core` dependency at pinned version *(fill in)*.
- Related working note in `external/dcl/notes/` *(fill in)*.
